git diff main..forge/v6.10.0 --name-only shows all 16 REWRITE paths modified. Each contains jq -n, jq -e, or bash =~ patterns confirming non-trivial rewrite. Harness reports 203/190/0 (all REWRITE scenarios PASS).

per_req_verdicts.description: Anti-pattern constraint: no awk+source in V6100_TOUCHED
per_req_verdicts.evidence: v6.9.0-pause-timeout-validation.sh uses REQ-T1-5 path (a) — inline redefine-and-test with comment 'NOT awk-extracted'. No V6100_TOUCHED scenario contains the awk+source pattern (confirmed by anti-pattern gate at v6.10.0-no-awk-source-in-rewrites.sh PASS).
v6.10.0-no-awk-source-in-rewrites.sh exists, has bash -n syntax-valid, TOUCHED_SCENARIOS array contains 50 entries (spec says 50 ± drift). Scenario passed in harness.

DISCREPANCY FOUND: git ls-tree v6.9.2 tests/scenarios/*.sh = 184 files (spec states baseline=185). git ls-tree forge/v6.10.0 tests/scenarios/*.sh = 203 (184 + 19 net-new). Spec equation: 185+19=204; actual: 184+19=203. The spec's stated baseline of 185 is off by 1 vs the actual v6.9.2 tag count of 184. The harness run during Phase 7 reports 203/190/0/13 — 203 total, not 204. Phase 7 wave 3 commit message says '203/190/0/13'. The AC-T1-9-1 hard-equality assertion of ==204 will FAIL at 203. REQ-T1-9 spec arithmetic error: spec cited baseline=185, actual tag has 184. Net result: total=203, not 204.
per_req_verdicts.severity: HIGH — hard equality assertion AC-T1-9-1 fails (203 ≠ 204)

per_req_verdicts.evidence: Section '## Functional test scenarios — security expectations' exists with exactly 7 items. Items 1-3 cover command substitution, eval, and out-of-tree sourcing. However, spec item 4 ('No awk+source code-lift pattern — cross-reference REQ-T1-5') is NOT a standalone item — it is merged into item 3 (out-of-tree sourcing). The spec's items 4-7 become CONTRIBUTING items 4-7 with a 1-position shift. Content coverage is present but item 4 (awk+source explicit prohibition with REQ-T1-5 cross-reference) is missing as a standalone bullet. Test AC-T1-13-2 only checks 4 phrases (eval, set -, fixtures.sh, trap) — not the awk+source phrase — so test passed despite this gap.
per_req_verdicts.severity: LOW — content intent preserved via item 3 merger; test passed

scope_drift_findings.description: Harness count = 203, not 204. Spec arithmetic used baseline=185 but v6.9.2 tag has 184 scenarios. The spec's stated baseline was off-by-1. Phase 7 harness output confirms 203 total. AC-T1-9-1 hard-equality assertion (==204) will fail. AC-T1-9-2 'PASS+SKIP+FAIL==204' will also fail (actual: PASS+SKIP+FAIL=203). This is the single most material spec-alignment gap.

CONTRIBUTING.md item 4 (explicit awk+source prohibition with REQ-T1-5 cross-reference) was not implemented as a standalone item. The anti-pattern is subsumed in item 3 (out-of-tree sourcing) with no explicit 'awk+source' mention or REQ-T1-5 cross-reference. Items 4-7 in CONTRIBUTING correspond to spec items 5-7 + trap, shifted by one. Functional intent preserved; standalone bullet absent.

Spec says '~33 person-hours' but CHANGELOG says '~30h'. Spec text also said 'informational (non-testable)' — the test only checks for the regex '~[0-9]+h' which matches ~30h. No hard violation but value differs from spec estimate.

score_rationale: 13/15 REQs fully fulfilled (0.867 ratio). Deduct 0.06 for REQ-T1-9 hard-equality count failure (203 vs 204 — spec arithmetic error propagated; HIGH severity). Deduct 0.03 for REQ-T1-13 awk+source item merger (LOW severity). Add 0.02 for strong Track 2 + Track 3 alignment. Score: 0.89.
confidence_rationale: Direct file-level verification performed for all sampled REQs. Counts verified via git ls-tree (authoritative). Research artifacts confirmed with full schema check. The 203 vs 204 discrepancy is unambiguous — traced to spec's stated baseline=185 vs actual tag count=184.
key_finding: The single material spec-alignment gap is REQ-T1-9: harness count is 203 (actual v6.9.2 baseline=184 + 19 net-new), not 204 (spec's stated baseline=185). The spec's baseline was misstated by 1. AC-T1-9-1 hard-equality assertion and AC-T1-9-2 combined-count assertion will both fail at the hard ==204 threshold. All other core REQs (Track 2, Track 3, research artifacts, CHANGELOG, roadmap corrections) are fully fulfilled.
